success_surface: Successful transposition of multi-value cell data from source sheet to target sheet with proper handling of missing values
effective_behaviors: Iterative row scanning with conditional value extraction for transformation tasks
Splitting compound cell values into discrete rows when data normalization is required
Preserving incomplete records with unfilled secondary fields rather than discarding them
Clearing target worksheet region before populating transformed data to avoid stale content
Using openpyxl directly for read-write operations when workbook structure must be maintained across sheets
Programmatic sheet access verification before attempting to read or write to specific worksheets
A1-style cell addressing with dynamic row enumeration for sequential data writing
evidence: Used iter_rows() with min_row/max_col parameters to systematically scan source columns
Applied string split('\n') on cell values to transform multi-value entries into separate rows
Checked if category exists before adding row, but added None for missing secondary values per instruction
Called ws.delete_rows() and ws.delete_cols() to clear target sheet before writing new data
Accessed wb.sheetnames to verify 'result' sheet existence before assignment
Saved final workbook to output_path without modifying the original init file

memory_items.title: Compound Cell Value Splitting
memory_items.lesson: When source cells contain multiple values (e.g., newline-separated), split them during extraction to normalize into separate rows.
memory_items.generalization: Detect compound value patterns in source cells and implement row duplication logic before target population.

memory_items.title: Incomplete Record Preservation
memory_items.lesson: Retain all valid primary records even when secondary/detailed fields are missing; use unfilled cells rather than omitting rows.
memory_items.generalization: Distinguish between missing keys (skip) and missing values (retain with nulls) based on task requirements.

memory_items.title: Target Region Sanitization
memory_items.lesson: Before writing transformed data to a target sheet or range, clear existing content to prevent data pollution.
memory_items.generalization: Implement explicit cleanup of target area using appropriate deletion methods before data insertion.

memory_items.title: Workbook-Wide Sheet Navigation
memory_items.lesson: When manipulating multiple sheets within one file, verify sheet availability programmatically and maintain workbook context throughout.
memory_items.generalization: Use wb.sheetnames checks and consistent workbook object reference for cross-sheet operations.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons

### Compound Cell Value Splitting
When source cells contain multiple values (e.g., newline-separated), split them during extraction to normalize into separate rows. Detect compound value patterns in source cells and implement row duplication logic before target population.

### Incomplete Record Preservation
Retain all valid primary records even when secondary/detailed fields are missing; use unfilled cells rather than omitting rows. Distinguish between missing keys (skip) and missing values (retain with nulls) based on task requirements.

### Target Region Sanitization
Before writing transformed data to a target sheet or range, clear existing content to prevent data pollution. Implement explicit cleanup of target area using appropriate deletion methods before data insertion.

### Workbook-Wide Sheet Navigation
When manipulating multiple sheets within one file, verify sheet availability programmatically and maintain workbook context throughout. Use wb.sheetnames checks and consistent workbook object reference for cross-sheet operations.
